channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: The war in Iran has left hundreds of U.S. diplomats in limbo
item.description: As the U.S-Iran war ramps up again, hundreds of diplomats are in limbo. The State Department drew down staffing due to Iran's attacks on Gulf states. It's unclear if they'll return to normal business.
item.content:encoded: As the U.S-Iran war ramps up again, hundreds of diplomats are in limbo. The State Department drew down staffing due to Iran's attacks on Gulf states. It's unclear if they'll return to normal business.
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen

item.title: At Mississippi funeral, Civil Rights leaders call for justice for Nolan Wells, 18
item.description: The parents of Nolan Wells, who was found dead on Horn Island in Mississippi, will hold a funeral for the 18-year-old on Monday. Wells was with friends for the Fourth of July and did not return home.
item.content:encoded:
The parents of Nolan Wells, who was found dead on Horn Island in Mississippi, will hold a funeral for the 18-year-old on Monday. Wells was with friends for the Fourth of July and did not return home.

(Image credit: Gerald Herbert)
item.dc:creator: Elise Gregg

item.title: World Cup: Spain's homecoming heroes
item.description: A nation celebrates as Spain's World Cup champions come home to a hero's welcome.
item.content:encoded: A nation celebrates as Spain's World Cup champions come home to a hero's welcome.
item.dc:creator: Miguel Macias

item.title: What led to the arrests of Andrew and Tristan Tate
item.description: Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan were arrested in Miami and now face extradition. New Yorker reporter Heidi Blake talks about the new rape and human trafficking charges from British prosecutors.
item.content:encoded: Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan were arrested in Miami and now face extradition. New Yorker reporter Heidi Blake talks about the new rape and human trafficking charges from British prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Ailsa Chang

item.title: The Trump administration said no kids died from USAID cuts. Consider these 3 cases
item.description: Some studies suggest tens of thousands have perished due to USAID disappearing. we bring two stories of children who got sick from usually preventable illnesses in areas where funding was reduced.
item.content:encoded: Some studies suggest tens of thousands have perished due to USAID disappearing. we bring two stories of children who got sick from usually preventable illnesses in areas where funding was reduced.
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: Trump says Qatari-donated Air Force One will be 'maxed out,' raising questions
item.description: The White House says the newest plane to serve as Air Force One will soon be sent away for upgrades. That has renewed questions about the security features of the jet that was donated by Qatar.
item.content:encoded: The White House says the newest plane to serve as Air Force One will soon be sent away for upgrades. That has renewed questions about the security features of the jet that was donated by Qatar.
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: Artist Bill Fontana explores the world's hidden sounds
item.description: Sound artist Bill Fontana creates works that often show the hidden music of everyday environments. His new work amplifies still bells at the Grace Cathedral in San Francisco.
item.content:encoded: Sound artist Bill Fontana creates works that often show the hidden music of everyday environments. His new work amplifies still bells at the Grace Cathedral in San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Buffy Gorrilla

item.title: What to expect from Britain's new prime minister, Andy Burnham
item.description: Andy Burnham is Britain's seventh prime minister in the past decade. He says he'll devolve power away from London and to regional and municipal authorities.
item.content:encoded: Andy Burnham is Britain's seventh prime minister in the past decade. He says he'll devolve power away from London and to regional and municipal authorities.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Who should regulate global AI? China makes a play for leadership
item.description: The AI world is moving at lightning speed – and the world is trying to figure out how to regulate this technology. China just made a big play to be the global leader.
item.content:encoded: The AI world is moving at lightning speed – and the world is trying to figure out how to regulate this technology. China just made a big play to be the global leader.
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: FDA retracts positive cyclospora test on Taylor Farms lettuce
item.description: The FDA says the test identifying cyclospora in Taylor Farms iceberg lettuce was a "false positive." But the lettuce is still linked epidemiologically to cases of the illness in a multistate outbreak.
item.content:encoded: The FDA says the test identifying cyclospora in Taylor Farms iceberg lettuce was a "false positive." But the lettuce is still linked epidemiologically to cases of the illness in a multistate outbreak.
item.dc:creator: Yuki Noguchi
